name: N3: [Supplies] VDD_0p8 drops to 0 after chip_en being reset
description:
**Why:** QS222 - needs to be validated with internal supplies as a power source.

**Deliverable:**
a) Complete debug studies and provide a clear working solution for the QS222 - validation with the internal supplies (0.8 and 1.2V) or aka SIMO output.

**Risks: (T) :** If QS 222 - cannot be validated with internal supply as power source, then design and architecture needs to come up with an alternative plan and provide a fix for the QS 222 validation. Fallback option is to use the external supply.

**Limitations: (C):** Design help for debug.
**(A):** Issue observed is replicated all the time and can provide insights to the problem observed.

**Results:** Anish / Aniruddh will post the debug studies results here.
creator: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: @Pushpal Das @Sarat Anumula why this task is still open? This task was long back done.
comments.author: Aniruddh CHoudhary

comments.text: @Pushpal Das @Sarat Anumula we should close this task as SIMO changes are incorporated from test results ran with Vinayak & Sumon (New task was open for the same purpose).
comments.author: Aniruddh CHoudhary

comments.text: Making it as open - because of the other higher priority tasks - with baseband testing.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: Thanks @Aniruddh CHoudhary for the note. Can you please list out any more DOEs that we can try out and resolve this issue.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: There are some finding in the debug although not fully resolved. Some supply connections were mixed (a few dig supplier were power by external 0.8V and while others by buck 0.8V). And due to that buck output was getting clamped.
comments.author: Aniruddh CHoudhary

comments.text: Pushing the due date to next week, as Anish is on vacation and also Aniruddh is also not available today.  Design needs to analyze in detail on the post silicon findings.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: @Aniruddh CHoudhary - Since Anish is on vacation, can you please a 1-2 line update on this task and what's needs to be done.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text:
This has been dragging because of the schedule implementation, which took much longer than planned. We have had a few discussion on why that happened, and will do written retroactive analysis once it's closed to ensure we plan better.

Generally, delay was caused by unclear up-front requirements and expectations, some IC bugs that were found for this, and some debugs for other IC features.
comments.author: Suhaib Ishaque

comments.text: @sumon @Kumaresh Dhotrad - Can you please update the due dates on this task? or provide any additional details here to reflect the latest status.
